reasoning: In November 2024, Starlink secured initial regulatory approval to operate in Chad, and Elon Musk celebrated the upcoming launch. In mid-March 2025, Starlink officially signed its operator license in Chad, finalizing the regulatory process. The question asks if the Starlink Map will show Chad as 'Available' before July 1, 2025. Since the license was just finalized, Starlink has ~3.5 months (mid-March to end of June) to officially make the service commercially available and update its map status. Given that Chad is long overdue (originally slated for 2024) and the Chadian government is eager for the service to roll out to solve internet reliability issues, the technical and legal hurdles seem to be cleared. The main risk is bureaucratic or logistical delays similar to previous African rollouts, but 3.5 months from definitive license signing to online ordering is a generous timeline for Starlink. I estimate a 75% probability of YES.
